assertions.expectation: Recommends combining release channels with maintenance exclusions as the approach for maximum control
assertions.reason: Response explicitly recommends 'Extended Channel + Persistent Exclusions' configuration combining --release-channel extended with --add-maintenance-exclusion-scope

assertions.expectation: Recommends 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion to prevent disruptive changes while allowing control plane security patches. May also mention persistent exclusions with --add-maintenance-exclusion-until-end-of-support (no longer a 6-month max, auto-tracks EoS)
assertions.reason: Response recommends --add-maintenance-exclusion-scope no_minor_or_node_upgrades and includes --add-maintenance-exclusion-until-end-of-support with detailed explanation of persistent tracking and EoS benefits

assertions.expectation: Recommends maintenance windows to control WHEN patches are applied (e.g., weekend windows only)
assertions.reason: Response specifies maintenance window configuration with Saturday 2-6 AM timing: --maintenance-window-start, --maintenance-window-duration 4h, and --maintenance-window-recurrence 'FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=SA'

assertions.expectation: Mentions the cluster disruption budget (disruption interval) to control upgrade frequency — patch interval default 7d (max 90d), minor interval default 30d (max 90d)
assertions.reason: Response includes --maintenance-patch-version-disruption-interval=7776000s (90 days) and explains how this controls patch frequency with alternatives like 30d, 45d, 60d, and discusses the 90-day maximum

assertions.expectation: Suggests Stable or Extended channel for production clusters in regulated environments. Extended channel provides maximum flexibility around EoS enforcement (additional cost only during extended period)
assertions.reason: Response recommends Extended channel for production and explains '24-month support instead of standard 14 months (extra cost only during extended period)' and 'Maximum flexibility around End of Support enforcement'

assertions.expectation: Notes that disabling control plane patches entirely is not recommended due to security risks — monthly patches at minimum
assertions.reason: Response emphasizes allowing security patches automatically and states 'No risk of falling behind on CVEs' and 'Security patches automated' while showing monthly patches as a moderate control option

assertions.expectation: May mention rollout sequencing as an advanced option for controlling upgrade order across multiple clusters, and/or the cluster disruption budget for controlling CP patch frequency
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'Rollout sequencing support' as an advantage and thoroughly covers cluster disruption budget via maintenance-patch-version-disruption-interval configuration

assertions.expectation: Provides gcloud commands for configuring the recommended exclusion type and maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response provides comprehensive gcloud commands including cluster update with maintenance exclusions, maintenance windows, and separate commands for manual upgrades when needed
